Hmm, maybe the goal is to position the character so that it forms a complete shape with the Y-shape. Together they form a wider P-shape.
But that doesn't seem like a clear goal. Let me think about this differently.
Maybe the game is about "collecting" the Y-shape by fully overlapping with it. Currently, the character only partially overlaps with the Y-shape. To fully overlap with the Y-shape, I would need to move the character LEFT so that its vertical bar aligns with the Y-shape's vertical bar. But the character's vertical bar is on the RIGHT side, while the Y-shape's vertical bar is on the LEFT side. So they can't fully overlap unless the character's shape changes.

The Y-shape's vertical bar is at rows 48-53, cols 51-53 (3 cells wide, 6 cells tall). The character's vertical bar is at rows 48-53, cols 57-59 (3 cells wide, 6 cells tall).
So the character and Y-shape form a combined shape that looks like a wider P-shape with two vertical bars.
Hmm, maybe the goal is to move the character so that the Y-shape is completely covered/absorbed. Let me try moving LEFT to see if the character can overlap more with the Y-shape.
Actually wait, the character's vertical bar is on the RIGHT side (cols 57-59), and the Y-shape's vertical bar is on the LEFT side (cols 51-53). If I move the character LEFT by 4 units (or about 1-2 moves), the character's vertical bar would move to cols 55-57 or 52-54, potentially overlapping with the Y-shape's vertical bar.

If I move LEFT twice, the character moves to cols 45-53. The character's vertical bar would be at cols 51-53, which is exactly where the Y-shape's vertical bar is! They would fully overlap.
